reasoning: The daily average temperature for Reims in early November typically falls around 7.5°C. For mid-November (Nov 16), the historical mean drops to roughly 6.5°C. For December 9 and February 7, it drops further to ~3.5°C-4.0°C. By climatological standards alone, the structural shift means it is less likely than 50% that these later mid-autumn/winter dates will be warmer than an average early November day. Based on the explicit instruction that uncertainty must strictly increase and probabilities for distant dates must mathematically regress toward 0.5, I have modeled the estimates to start at 0.35 for the highly predictable shorter-horizon date (1 week), reverting incrementally up to 0.42 (1 month) and 0.47 (3 months) for the distant dates. This successfully balances the underlying expectation of seasonal cooling with the mandated horizon-based uncertainty structure.
